scope: AgenC memory extraction uses the source-aligned auto-memory prompt and a closed memory-type taxonomy at the declared PR-08 target surface.

requiredBehaviors: The extraction prompt tells the child to use only the most recent model-visible messages.
Existing memory manifests are included only when non-empty and tell the child to update existing files before creating duplicates.
The prompt lists only the tools allowed by the extraction child policy.
edgeCases: Empty or whitespace-only manifests do not render an empty Existing memory files section.

requiredBehaviors: Memory extraction uses the closed user, feedback, project, and reference taxonomy.
Unknown or missing frontmatter types degrade gracefully.
The what-not-to-save section is AgenC-branded and excludes derivable code, git history, existing AGENC.md content, and ephemeral task state.
edgeCases: The taxonomy frontmatter example remains synchronized with the parseable memory type set.

requiredBehaviors: Default extraction writes a topic file and then updates MEMORY.md as an index.
Index-disabled extraction writes topic files without instructing the child to update MEMORY.md.
edgeCases: MEMORY.md is described as an index, not a memory file.

requiredBehaviors: The old memdir production boundary contains only the PR-08 memory type target and deferred team-memory files.
edgeCases: Additional production files under runtime/src/memdir fail the memory contract test.
